summary: Fixed MCP server startup errors on Windows by wrapping commands in cmd.exe and increasing the connection timeout.
root_cause: Node.js CVE-2024-27980 restricts spawning .cmd files without shell: true. Using `StdioClientTransport` without it threw `ENOENT` or `EINVAL`. Also, `npx -y` downloads the packages on first run, which exceeded the 15-second connection timeout.
fix_applied: Modified `src/core/pluginRegistry.ts` to wrap MCP server commands in `cmd.exe /d /s /c` on Windows and increased the timeout from 15s to 60s.
proposed_skill_update: On Windows, when dynamically spawning MCP servers using `npx` or `uvx` through `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/stdio`, always prepend `cmd.exe /c` to bypass `ENOENT` and Node CVE `EINVAL` restrictions, and set a timeout >= 60 seconds to allow package installation.

original_task: Agent stuck in infinite loop calling server_info/notebook_list repeatedly
summary: Fixed false-positive failure loop detection that caused the agent to enter infinite recovery spirals on successful MCP tool calls.
root_cause: SelectionActor's loop detection used broad `h.result.includes('Error')` string matching on JSON-serialized tool results. Successful MCP responses like `{status: 'success', output: {content: [{text: '...Error count: 0...'}]}}` contained the word 'Error' in their output, triggering the CRITICAL WARNING recovery prompt. This confused the LLM into re-calling diagnostic tools endlessly instead of synthesizing the answer it already had.
fix_applied: Three surgical changes to `src/nodes/executionNodes.ts`: (1) Replaced string matching with structured JSON `status === 'error'` checks in failure detection. (2) Added MCP output sanitization to extract clean text from CallToolResult objects before storing in actionHistory. (3) Added redundant-success detection that forces task completion when the same tool succeeds 2x with identical args. (4) Added prompt-level instruction telling the LLM to check history before calling new tools.
proposed_skill_update: When detecting failure loops in agentic systems, NEVER use broad string matching on tool outputs. Always parse the structured status field first. Reserve string heuristics for unambiguous markers only (PAGE_LOAD_FAILED, robots.txt).
